title: Conical Burr Coffee Grinder Electric, Upgrade Anti-static Electric Espresso Coffee Bean Grinder for Home Use,48 Precise Grind Settings for 2-20 Cups, Stainless Steel
description:
About this item
【48 Precise Grinding Settings】: The 48 grind options allow for a wide range of adjustment of the gap between the internal burrs,fully ensuring that the grinding of each setting is suitable for different coffee cooking, From the finest Espresso to the coarsest French Press Grind.
【Premium Conical Burr】: Conical burrs grind coffee more evenly than flat burrs, which prevents the taste from being too light or astringent. The conical burr design helps minimizes grinding heat and retains the best flavor and texture of coffee beans. The grinding process is also much quieter, bringing you a better grinding experience.
【Upgrade Anti-static Coffee Grinder Electric】: Anti-static technology makes coffee chamber mess-free, preventing coffee grounds from flying around and messing up the worktop.
【Large Capacity Bean Hopper】: Bean hopper Holds up to 12 oz. for 2-20 cups. And there are seconds marks from 5-40 second above the timing knob to suit your grinding needs.
【Block-proof Design】: The Conical burrs grinder coffee built-in cleaning brush, which can effectively clean the internal powder to avoid powder blockage, and the brush included can clean removable upper burr.
【Durable Stainless Steel Material】: Crafted from high-quality stainless steel, the TWOMEOW coffee grinder is both durable and stylish, making it an elegant addition to any kitchen space.
【After-Sales Service】 TWOMEOW Conical burr coffee grinder Electric has a professional after-sales service team. If you have any questions, you can contact us at any time, we will help you in time.
reviews_5s:
Received this grinder and the Shardor grinder with electronic timer and readout at the same time to compare. Both are nice grinders and appear to have very similar or identical grinding mechanisms -including the way in which the top burr is removed for cleaning. Both have a max 40 second time setting which I tested on the Twomeow and found it to be very accurate: 20 second setting runs for 20 seconds exactly. Both have a stainless steel housing and their footprints are also almost the same (within 1/2" or 12mm). I decided to keep this grinder because of the following factors: It runs a little quieter (although both are very quiet), has a bigger hopper and a bigger receptacle for the grounds. The main grind settings appear to be a good match to my French Press as well as the drip coffee maker with either a paper filter or a reusable mesh filter. The anti-static function works amazingly well: a little tapping when you pour the grounds, and the container is totally empty and clean. I just hope that whatever is used to eliminate the static is not some chemical that turns out to be toxic (there are different methods to eliminate or reduce static buildup, and I have not found the specific technology for this grinder.) I replaced a twice as expensive Bodum grinder that broke after less than two years of use, and this grinder appears to be of better quality all around. I can't speak to the longevity of this machine, but I would expect it do perform well in the longer term.I replaced a straight burr grinder. This one is much quieter. And for me the biggest benefit is that there has been no noticeable static coffee grounds spread all over.
The ground coffee is ground consistently. The range of grind is from power to rough. The controls are simple.
It looks good and is quite a space saver as it is deeper than it is wide.This is a conical grinder, and I replaced a straight burr grinder with this one. This one is so much better and far quieter than the other one. I was so impressed that I could do other things in the kitchen while it ground because it stops at 40 seconds. Also I love the no static feature in the cup. It is also much, much cleaner in our coffee area now. I noticed that it is quieter than the grinder at the store. If I need to grind in the morning it doesn't wake my partner (or neighbors). I highly recommend this grinder, but keep in mind I've only had it for about two weeks, so I really hope it goes for a long time.Very good burr coffee grinder. It's true the fresh grounds barely stick to the inside of the receiver. Very simple operation. I've used it for several weeks now and it seems well made. Hopefully the durability will show through over the next few years. I took a chance and so far am pleased!Great grinds. Grinds so much faster and quieter. Like the way the grind container is made. For standard espresso, I grind at 8 for 0.3mm and 12 for 0.4mm sieves (non-pressurized). Pressurized shots I grind at 8. I grind for 20 seconds max with 10 seconds in between to prevent adverse grind heat. Usually a double shot is fully ground in less than 20 seconds. I grind drip coffee at 24-32.

Standard 12 cup carafe can 6 scoop will fill this grind receptacle. Empty before grinding more.Been using for a month now and I’m amazed by the anti static function actually working and zero coffee grounds messing up the kitchen. I’m also impressed with how quiet the grinder is on various settings! Going to buy another one for a Christmas gift, absolutely the best coffee grinder I’ve owned over 41 years.REally enjoying this burr grinder; there's nothing better than a fresh cup of coffee from fresh ground beans!Easy to set up and use. Everything worked right out of the box. The hopper holds a out a half pound of beans The timer is is simple to use. Took a little trial and error to figure out the appropriate amount for a 1L French press. The grind levels are accurate. Coffee came out at the right strength.Solid and as expected.Very quiet. Have owned several grinders and this is by far the best. Highly reccomend.
reviews_1s: It would run fine without any beans in it. As soon as I tried to add beans, it would run for a second and shut off. Troubleshoot claimed jammed coffee beans. I cleaned it out and it just kept happening over and over againThe grinder did not work from the start. The motor worked but none of the beans went through. Returned.This lasted less than 2 months! They claim a 2 year warranty but do not respond when contacting them.
